One Link
How it works Features Security Share Get One Link
Features

Everything One Link can do, told by One Link.

This page is generated from the live capability advertisement of the One Link daemon at /api/capabilities. We cannot lie on this page. If a feature is here, the daemon ships it. If it is not, it does not.

live from /api/capabilities updated 2s ago
For people

The everyday things.

Messages

Only you and they can read it. Forward secrecy. Edit, delete, react. Read receipts that you control.

Files of any size

Drag a folder. Drag a movie. Drag a hard drive's worth. It goes peer to peer, resumes on reconnect, never copies to a server.

Voice and video calls

End to end encrypted. Direct peer to peer when possible, relayed only if needed. No mystery server in the path.

Shared folders

Drop a file in, it appears on the other side. Versioned, conflict-free, stored on your devices.

Pair a device in five seconds

QR plus five words. The five words mean the math agreed. That is the whole pairing flow.

Your devices act as one

Phone, laptop, tablet, second laptop. Pair once. They share everything, end to end, between every device.

For privacy

Things only One Link does.

Three-hop private routing (in flight, lands v0.22)

One toggle. Sphinx-routed through volunteer relays. Post-quantum blinded at every hop. No one knows who sent what to whom. The crates are shipped today; the daemon toggle wires in v0.22.

Hardware key recognition

Your device recognizes itself when you return, without you signing in, without us storing a single thing about you.

Threshold recovery

Split a secret across friends. Recover it from any threshold. Your account survives a lost phone without surviving a leak.

Field-bound binding

Each session is bound to the physical environment. A stolen key cannot be replayed on a different network without detection.

Duress mode (in flight, lands v0.22)

A second passphrase that opens a believable empty account. The math gives you a way out. Primitive crate shipped; daemon wiring lands in v0.22.

Confidential builds

Every release is built in a sealed environment with attestation. You can verify the binary matches the attested build.

The promises, kept by the architecture

What One Link guarantees.

Each line is a property the math itself enforces, not a policy someone could change later. The crate or proof behind each one is named, linkable, and audit-ready.

No account, no email, no phone number.

Identity is a keypair on your device. There is no user database anywhere. Crate: ol_pair_qr. Identifier rotation built into the protocol.

End-to-end encrypted by default.

The cleartext never leaves your device. Every chunk, every message. Crates: ol_ratchet, ol_transfer. Per-message forward secrecy active today; per-chunk activation lands with the chunk-store cutover in v0.22.

No central server in the path.

Direct peer-to-peer when possible, encrypted-relay forwarding when not. The relays we operate are just nodes; the network keeps running if every one of them goes away. Anyone can run a relay.

Post-quantum hybrid signatures live everywhere.

Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 hybrid for every signature today. X25519 + ML-KEM-768 hybrid keys live on Sphinx onion hops today; the pair handshake and channel root activate the ML-KEM half in v0.22 (the wire format already reserves the slot). If either half of the hybrid breaks, the other still holds. Crates: ol_pqkem, ol_pqsig, ol_onion.

Reproducible signed builds.

Every release is built in a sealed environment with an attestation document. Anyone can rebuild from source and verify byte equality. Verify in your tab on the download page.

Send anything peer-to-peer, no size cap.

Native chunked transfer with per-chunk AEAD and fountain-coded loss recovery. 10 GB transfers verified in the torture simulator. Crate: ol_transfer.

Works on LAN or offline.

The protocol is mDNS- and Bluetooth-aware. Pair across a room, send across an air-gapped network, sync when you reconnect.

Source open and verifiable.

AGPL-3.0. Every crate, every shader, every word of this site. github.com/IamOneYouAreOneWeAreOne/one-link

Pair devices in five seconds.

QR plus a five-word short-authentication-string. The words mean the math agreed. Crate: ol_pair_qr. Levenshtein-audited wordlist.

Free forever, no tier, no upsell.

No business model. No subscription. No "pro" anything. The network is community-run, and so is the project.

Threshold key recovery.

Split your account across k of n trusted shares. Survive a lost device without surviving a leak. Crate: ol_threshold_recovery. BN multi-sig with per-signer R values.

Confidential-compute attestation.

The build proves where it ran and what it ran. Crate: ol_confidential. Software baseline live today. Windows TPM provider in flight. The trait surface admits macOS Secure Enclave, Linux SGX/SEV-SNP, and Android TrustZone providers in future cycles.

Last reviewed 2026-05-17.

Get One Link → Read the threat model
One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.
